content.assessment: By design this proposal leaves the selection age, the track structure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01), the centralized exam (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02), and the statutory free-choice right (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03) completely unchanged, making it the lowest-friction option institutionally. However, this same non-interference means the specific governance and selection mechanisms that lens evidence ties to sorting outcomes — exam-based entry and coaching-sensitive preparation (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05), and the documented parental-education composition gap (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) — continue operating exactly as before. The proposal is compatible with existing administration but does not interact with the institutional mechanisms this lens is built to examine; it is orthogonal to selection and governance rather than reforming them.
opportunities: Could operate in parallel with the ministry's own diagnosis that 6/8-grade advantage stems from favorable family background rather than value-added learning (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08), by targeting resources at schools outside the selective track without contesting the selective structure itself
strengths: Fully compatible with existing administrative structures since no institutional mechanism identified in the evidence (tracks, exams, choice rights) is altered
Can be implemented without confronting the free-choice guarantee the minister considers non-negotiable (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03), reducing legal-political friction
threats: Because the institutional sorting point remains fully active, EU-topping segregation outcomes (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06) are likely to persist unaffected by compensatory funding alone, from a purely institutional-interaction perspective
Feeder-school creaming dynamics shown in the Encs case (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07) indicate that composition effects operate through governance channels this proposal does not engage
weaknesses: Does not touch the admission exam or coaching dynamics documented as a channel of family-capital advantage (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02, F-live-hungarian_education_system-05)
Leaves the parental-education composition gap between track types (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) fully intact, since no selection or governance mechanism is modified
